2606.25978.title: Multi-Agent Goal Recognition with Team- and Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning and Factorized Branch-and-Bound
2606.25978.paper_summary: Multi-agent goal recognition asks an observer to jointly infer which agents act together and what each team is trying to achieve, so the hypothesis space grows combinatorially with the number of team partitions and goals per team. Real applications such as drone surveillance and collaborative robotics expose only the agents' trajectory, which forces the observer to rank team-goal hypotheses from behavior alone. Multi-Agent Goal Recognition with Branch-and-Bound (MAGR-BB) addresses this setting with a shared team- and goal-conditioned policy used as the scoring model inside a factorized branch-and-bound search. On a controlled multi-agent Blocksworld benchmark, MAGR-BB returns the same top-ranked hypothesis as exhaustive search throughout the trajectory while cutting hypothesis materialization by orders of magnitude and reducing cumulative recognition runtime substantially.
2606.25978.paper_summary_zh: 多代理目標識別要求觀察者共同推斷哪些代理一起行動以及每個團隊試圖達成什麼，因此假設空間隨著團隊劃分和每個團隊的目標數量的增加而以組合方式增長。實際應用如無人機監控和協作機器人僅暴露代理的軌跡，這迫使觀察者僅根據行為對團隊-目標假設進行排名。帶有分支限界的多代理目標識別（MAGR-BB）在這種情況下使用共享的團隊和目標條件策略作為因子化分支限界搜索中的評分模型。在一個受控的多代理 Blocksworld 基準測試中，MAGR-BB 在整個軌跡中返回與全面搜索相同的最高排名假設，同時將假設實現的數量減少了數個量級，並顯著減少了累積識別運行時間。
2606.25978.author: Thiago Thomas et.al.
2606.25978.authors: Thiago Thomas, Gabriel de Oliveira Ramos, Felipe Meneguzzi
